title: French artist Olympe Ramakrishna’s exhibition in Thiruvananthapuram celebrates Malayali women away from home
content: Voices of the Western Coast exhibition displays on fabric the portrait of three Malayali mothers and their daughters who reside outside the State

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Mamata releases TMC manifesto, claims 'unofficial President's Rule' imposed in poll-bound Bengal
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma files nomination from Assam's Jalukbari constituency; Mr. Sarma laid down his objective for Assam, centred around the identity and development of Assam

title: Indian killed in Riyadh due to ‘recent events of March 18’, says Embassy
content: The Indian embassy said that it is in contact with the deceased’s family and local authorities but the circumstances regarding the Indian national’s death remain unclear

title: Uttam Nagar violence: Mehbooba Mufti seeks PM Modi's intervention ahead of Id
content: Mehbooba Mufti urged the Prime Minister and Union Home Minister Amit Shah to intervene "before the situation worsens, leading to loss of innocent lives.”

title: CJI to shift challenge to 2023 CEC appointment law to another Bench
content: Advocate Prashant Bhushan, appearing for one of the petitioners, Association for Democratic Reforms, suggests the case be shifted to a Bench that does not have a prospective Chief Justice of India either as the lead or associate judge.

title: ‘Aadu 3’ movie review: Flogging a dead goat through multiple timelines
content: Despite its ambitious scope and comedic potential, the nearly three-hour movie only offers a series of character introductions rather than a cohesive narrative, ultimately leaving viewers yearning for a more coherent narrative and a satisfying conclusion

title: Telangana Budget 2026-27 LIVE: Govt introduces ₹3.24 lakh crore budget, public debt inching to ₹6 lakh
content: Panchayat Raj and Rural Development, Municipal Administration and Urban Development, Irrigation and other sectors get highest allocations

title: Why has Madras High Court ordered eradication of seemai karuvelam? | Explained
content: Why is prosopis juliflora considered harmful? When did the court begin to crack the whip? What prevented the implementation of a series of orders? How is the new set of directions different from previous orders?

title: EC directs political parties to get advertisements pre-certified
content: Besides, the Commission has directed candidates to share the details of their authentic social media accounts in their affidavits at the time of filing nomination; individual candidates can apply for certification of advertisements with District Media Certification and Monitoring Committees

title: Kerala polls: Amid reports of rift in Congress, V.D. Satheesan says seat-sharing done without discord
content: Kerala’s Opposition Leader says UDF will return to power with ‘over 100 seats’ despite ‘sustained campaign of fake news’ targeting the Congress

title: Guindy Children’s Park closed after avian flu outbreak
content: Nearly 40 birds died in the facility since March 13 and samples sent to the National Institute of High Security Animal Diseases (NIHSAD) in Bhopal confirmed the presence of the H5N1 virus. The park will remain closed until results consistently return negative.

title: ED attaches ₹5,046.91-crore assets in PACL case
content: The agency has thus far attached movable and immovable properties valued at approximately ₹22,656.91 crore, including assets located both in India and abroad in this case

title: Upper age limit for Tamil Nadu Home Guards raised to 60 years
content: Following representations to revise the upper age limit for appointment to the Home Guards, the rules were amended and notified by the Home Department earlier this month

title: Bhatti presents welfare oriented 2026-27 budget for Telangana with emphasis on urban and rural development
content: Panchayat Raj and Rural Development, Municipal Administration and Urban Development, Irrigation and other sectors get highest allocations

title: Kerala polls: Meeting between K. Sudhakaran, Mohanan signals unity after Congress’ Kannur candidature row
content: Sudhakaran says he has ‘honoured the assurance’ given to the party and that he will work from the front to ensure Congress’ victory

title: Fight against hate speech must be on behalf of all, and not just limited to one’s own community: Supreme Court
content: Justice Nagarathna said the court was against the phenomenon of hate speech, whether it was targeted at one or the other community

title: Placed under house arrest, not allowed to offer Friday prayers, says Mirwaiz
content: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq also posted pictures purportedly showing a large contingent of security forces and police vehicles outside the gate of his residence in Nigeen locality of the city here.

title: Telangana Budget 2026-27: Government Announces cashless health security scheme for employees, teachers, and Pensioners Welfare
content: This cashless health security scheme (Employees Health Scheme-EHS) will be implemented through the Rajiv Aarogyasri Trust

title: Tourist rescued after falling more than 100 feet in Nilgiris while taking selfie
content: The tourist, identified as Sivagurunathan, fell on Thursday (March 19, 2026) evening and could not be rescued immediately as he had suffered fractures that left him incapacitated

title: Telangana Budget 2026-27: Government fears migration due to VB-G-RAM-G
content: “Current Central Government has brought in a new scheme called VB-G RAM G in place of MGNREGA. Our Telangana Legislative Assembly has unanimously opposed this new law,” says Deputy Chief Minister

title: Telangana Budget 2026-27| Allocations to farm, irrigation, energy sectors remain unchanged
content: Despite stupendous performance being put up by the Agriculture sector, the allocation for it is pruned to ₹23,179 for 2026-27 from ₹24,439 crore

title: Telangana Budget 2026-27: ₹11907 crore allocated to Home Department for Maoist rehabilitation
content: “The previous government and the present Central Government treated Maoism merely as a law-and-order problem and tried to suppress it through police and armed forces,” says Deputy Chief Minister

title: Power Minister stresses energy security amid global tensions
content: Stressing the importance of energy security amid global uncertainties, he (Lal) underlined the need to enhance per capita energy consumption and accelerate the transition towards renewable energy," a Power Ministry statement said.

title: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan slams CPI(M) defectors, cites rise of ‘parliamentary ambitions’
content: ‘They (G. Sudhakaran and T.K. Govindan) have destroyed their long political legacy. In our view, this amounts to betrayal — joining hands with rivals to weaken the party,’ says Pinarayi

title: Bengaluru’s KIA named best regional airport in India and south Asia; moves to 41st spot in global rankings
content: While Terminal 2 became the first in India to receive a 5-star Skytrax rating, the airport has also achieved the highest Level 5 Accreditation under ACI’s Airport Carbon, the BIAL said.

title: Supreme Court grants regular bail to Muppidi Avinash Reddy, an accused in Andhra liquor 'scam' case
content: The court said the regular bail would continue during the pendency of the trial proceedings in the case

title: Telangana Budget 2026-27: BRS legislators stage protest demanding Congress’s ‘six guarantees’ poll promise
content: They displayed placards in the name of “debt cards” with slogans on the unkept promises made as part of the Six Guarantees and a large number of other promises made by the Congress party before the 2023 Assembly elections

title: APCRDA job mela on March 25
content: The event scheduled to be organised at the Skill Hub in Thulluru is expected to fill over 452 vacancies across multiple sectors

title: Mangaluru-Moodbidri NH 169 portion near Yedapadavu opens for traffic after month-long closure
content: The National Highways Authority of India has completed construction of one of the two-carriageways of Yedapadavu bypass facilitating traffic movement

title: Runaway couple protection plea mentioned in Supreme Court, CJI asks lawyer to move Delhi HC
content: The lawyer said the couple came to the Supreme Court under the mistaken belief, influenced by social media reels, that they could get married within the court premises and receive immediate protection from the Chief Justice of India

title: Supreme Court allows bail to former police officer Rajesh Das in sexual harassment case
content: In April 2024, the High Court had dismissed a petition filed by Rajesh Das seeking exemption from surrendering in the case

title: Decisions of Opposition party in Tamil Nadu are taken by Delhi, alleges CM Stalin
content: The statement from Mr. Stalin came amid AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami’s visit to New Delhi and his meeting with Union Home Minister Amit Shah for seat-sharing negotiations

title: Union Minister Jayant Chaudhary receives death threat, caller traced to Bengal’s Murshidabad
content: Police said that according to preliminary investigation, the caller has been identified as Mohammad Ismail, a resident of Murshidabad in West Bengal.

title: Collection of names of animals as portrayed in Tamil classics
content: The ancient Tamils employed a remarkable richness of vocabulary to refer to animals. H. Chithiraputhiran, a former professor at Tamil University, Thanjavur, has compiled a specialised dictionary of animals in classical literature to recover these words

title: The unique flavour of Dindigul cigar, a balm to Winston Churchill during World War
content: During the Second World War, the British Prime Minister decided to have a regular supply of these cigars made by Spencer and Co. A permanent order was placed with the Government of Madras. Spencer and Co. shut the business in the 1950s. What is left of the Spencer Compound is a portion of the ground that has become an unkempt garden

title: Justice Indu Malhotra and K.K. Venugopal join expert panel to guide NCERT curriculum on Legal Studies
content: The Union Government’s top law officer orally submitted that the expert panel will work with J. Aniruddha Bose of the National Judicial Academy to finalise the curriculum for Class 8 and above

title: Ruckus in Odisha Assembly over demand for Health Minister’s resignation following hospital fire
content: Holding placards and shouting anti-government slogans, the Opposition members trooped into the well of the House and demanded the resignation of the Minister, holding the State government responsible for the death of 12 patients at SCB Medical College and Hospital in Cuttack.

title: Kerala polls: G. Sudhakaran says will campaign against Minister R. Prasad in Cherthala
content: Sudhakaran accuses the Minister of making ‘unwarranted remarks’. He rules out the possibility of a third Pinarayi Vijayan-led government in Kerala

title: Why alternatives can’t replace LPG yet in Karnataka
content: Renewable energy accounts for a larger share than conventional sources in Karnataka’s total installed power generation capacity. Yet, the LPG crisis exposed our continued dependence on conventional sources. The Hindu explores why it is so and what it takes to change.

title: TMC MP moves High Court over transfer of IAS, IPS officers by ECI
content: The petition, which questions the transfers without consulting the State government before the ECI took the decisions, is likely to be heard early next week, a lawyer connected with the matter said.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly elections | Seat-sharing to be finalised in four days: Palaniswami
content: Making it clear that the composition of the alliance had been more or less firmed up, Edappadi K. Palaniswami said AIADMK’s manifesto would be released in a week

title: Uttarakhand CM Pushkar Singh Dhami expands cabinet, inducts five MLAs
content: With the induction of the new Ministers — Khazan Das, Bharat Singh Chaudhary, Madan Kaushik, Pradeep Batra, and Ram Singh Kaida — the Uttarakhand cabinet has reached its maximum strength of 12, officials said

title: Former CPI(M) leader in Kerala booked for alleged sexual relations with woman on false marriage promise
content: The matter came to light after the 32-year-old woman reportedly attempted suicide after the accused allegedly withdrew from his promise to marry her, say police

title: Watch: Thoothukudi murder case: Did the system fail a 17-year-old girl? | Focus Tamil Nadu
content: A 17-year-old schoolgirl from Kulathur in Thoothukudi district was found murdered hours after going missing, raising serious questions about police response and delay in registering the complaint. This disturbing Tamil Nadu case highlights alleged police lapses, including jurisdiction issues and insensitive assumptions, sparking protests and a probe into possible systemic failure.

title: Supreme Court quashes copyright case against filmmaker Sujoy Ghosh over his 'Kahaani 2' film
content: The Supreme Court bench allowed Sujoy Ghosh's plea, challenging a Jharkhand High Court's order, which had refused to quash the proceedings pending against him.

title: Babri mosque emotion: West Bengal may see Muslim Chief Minister or Deputy CM: Humayun Kabir
content: Humayun Kabir said that his outfit would contest 182 seats in alliance with AIMIM and could emerge as a decisive player in government formation if the verdict produces a fractured mandate

title: LPG crisis: In Kerala, war reaches the doorsteps
content: The war that broke out in West Asia has impacted lives in Kerala in myriad forms. Expatriates’ travel plans were in disarray, tourism hit the pause button. But it was the unprecedented LPG crisis that put the economy under heavy strain. M.P. Praveen finds out how various segments, from restaurants to rubber industry, are coping with the situation

title: Luka drops 60, gets MVP chants from Heat crowd
content: Luka Doncic scored 60 points, the first Laker to do so since Kobe Bryant's career finale in 2016, as L.A. won in Miami to push its season-best win streak to eight games.

title: UNC's Davis: Fatigue not to blame for collapse
content: In a series of terse exchanges with reporters after VCU's 82-78 OT win, North Carolina coach Hubert Davis said he didn't think his team was tired and said it was "my decision" when asked why he had gone with a six-man rotation in the waning minutes.

title: LeBron ties Parish's record with 1,611th game
content: LeBron James tied Robert Parish for the NBA's all-time games-played mark, with the Hall of Famer saying James is "deserving" of breaking the record.

title: Duke dodges scare, tips cap to McNamara, Siena
content: Duke barely avoided a shocking upset to open the NCAA tournament, with the No. 1 overall seed having to rally from 13 points down and going ahead for good in the final five minutes to beat 16th-seeded Siena 71-65 on Thursday.

title: Sources: NFL to vote on Raiders succession plan
content: NFL owners are scheduled to vote on a Raiders succession plan that gives Silver Lake co-chief executive officer Egon Durban the option to purchase a majority stake in the team from longtime owner Mark Davis, sources told ESPN.

title: Wemby's game winner puts Spurs back in playoffs
content: Victor Wembanyama nailed a go-ahead 17-foot fadeaway with 1.1 seconds left Thursday night, and the Spurs clinched a postseason berth for the first time in six seasons.

title: BYU's Dybantsa to mull future in upcoming weeks
content: BYU freshman AJ Dybantsa said he expects to decide on his playing future in the "next couple of weeks."

title: NYU's 91-game win streak ends in D-III Final Four
content: Two-time defending champion NYU lost 60-52 to Scranton in the Final Four of the Division III NCAA tournament on Thursday night, ending the Violets' 91-game winning streak.

content: ESPN talent cast their votes for which foursome will reach the Final Four. Will Phoenix be full of chalk?

content: e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective . first day first show news and reviews from the world of cinema and streaming . the weekly newsletter from science writers takes the jargon out of science and puts the fun in!

title: Indian national dies in Saudi Arabia: MEA says 6 deaths so far amid Middle East tensions
content: Tragedy struck Riyadh as an Indian national lost their life on March 18th amidst escalating regional tensions. The Indian Embassy in Saudi Arabia has confirmed the demise and is actively supporting the deceased's family while coordinating with local authorities.

title: AB de Villiers reveals ‘naughty’ desire in his, Virat Kohli’s career downfall
content: For over a decade, Virat Kohli and AB de Villiers were RCB’s heartbeat, yet an IPL trophy eluded them. De Villiers reflected that their shared “naughty” trait, wanting to win too much, was both their strength and downfall, making losses feel personal. Their intense competitiveness forged legendary partnerships but came with heavy emotional tolls, highlighting the fine line between passion and pressure.

title: US F-35 stealth jet hit: How Iran’s infrared systems spooked the ‘ghost of the skies’
content: An F-35 stealth fighter was reportedly damaged and forced to land after being hit by Iranian fire during a combat mission over Iran. Analysts suggest Iran's infrared-guided air defense systems, which track heat signatures rather than radar, may have played a role in targeting the advanced aircraft.

title: Google is shaking up its AI team, & the reason is Nvidia CEO's newest obsession
content: Google is shifting resources from its browser-based AI, Project Mariner, as the industry embraces a new direction.  The focus is now on text-based AI agents, like OpenClaw, which offer superior speed and efficiency.  This pivot reflects a broader trend, with major AI players also prioritizing terminal-friendly agents for their future strategies.

title: 'Weak link Rahane, costly decisions' could spell disaster for KKR, warns Ex-IPL star
content: Former CSK batter S Badrinath has expressed significant concerns for KKR ahead of IPL 2026, labeling captain Ajinkya Rahane a "weak link" and questioning the team's leadership and costly squad decisions. Badrinath also criticized the record Rs 25.20 crore signing of Cameron Green, citing his recent poor form and questioning his value.

title: 'Dhurandhar : The Revenge': Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 250 crore global mark
content: The Indian box office is witnessing a historic moment as Aditya Dhar and Ranveer Singh’s 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has already surged to a cumulative domestic net of Rs. 165.36 crore by Friday afternoon. Despite an ‘A’ rating and a massive 3-hour 49-minute runtime, the film is on track to cross the Rs. 230 crore global gross mark by the end of Day 2.

title: Years after Zuckerberg called it 'the next frontier’ for company, Meta shuts down Horizon worlds
content: Meta is ending its virtual reality social network Horizon Worlds on Quest headsets. The app will be removed from the Quest store by March 2026 and fully discontinued on VR devices by June 2026. Horizon Worlds will then operate solely as a mobile application.

title: Mohsin Naqvi’s PCB termed ‘weak’, accused of ‘kneeling before players’
content: Pakistan cricket faces renewed criticism after a T20 World Cup exit and ODI series loss. Former opener Ahmed Shehzad alleges the PCB built up a select group of players, now failing to deliver, while neglecting accountability and player development. He claims the board's weakness empowers these stars, hindering genuine progress and leaving fans disillusioned.

title: FBI update on Iran-linked hackers who brought down US largest medical device company
content: The FBI has dismantled four Iranian intelligence-linked websites used for cyberattacks, data leaks, and threats against dissidents and US residents. These domains facilitated psychological operations, including claiming credit for a major medical device firm's hack and publishing sensitive information of Israeli officials. This action underscores a commitment to combating cyber threats and transnational repression.

title: Price of premium petrol hiked by up to Rs 2.35 amid Middle East tensions
content: Premium petrol prices have seen a hike of over Rs 2 per litre across state-run oil companies, while regular fuel and diesel rates remain steady. This adjustment comes amidst global energy market volatility fueled by the Middle East crisis, impacting India's significant oil import dependence and potentially widening the import bill.

title: Candace Owens says she was closest to Charlie Kirk amid row
content: Candace Owens has sparked fresh controversy by claiming Charlie Kirk was unaware of key details during his relationship with Erika Kirk. She alleges Erika misrepresented connections to Kanye West’s circle, raising questions about credibility and hidden truths. The remarks have triggered intense online debate, with critics demanding evidence while supporters cite Owens’ proximity as reason to take her claims seriously.

title: Safe passage for $2m? Iran sets up 'corridor' for ships to transit Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran has established a new safe shipping corridor in the Strait of Hormuz. Only ships with prior approval from Iranian authorities can use this route. Several countries are in talks with Tehran for their vessels. This controlled passage offers an alternative amid ongoing regional disruptions. Some Indian ships have already used this supervised route.

title: 'Corruption in judiciary' row: Centre forms panel to rewrite NCERT class 8 chapter
content: A three-member expert committee will rewrite the Class 8 NCERT textbook chapter on the judiciary. This follows a controversy over a section mentioning corruption in the judiciary. The Supreme Court has closed its proceedings after the government's assurance. NCERT had previously withdrawn the chapter. The committee includes K. K. Venugopal, Indu Malhotra, and Justice Aniruddha Bose.

title: Happy Eid-ul-Fitr: Best Eid mubarak messages, quotes, wishes, and images
content: Eid-ul-Fitr 2026 will soon arrive, bringing joy and marking the end of Ramadan. Celebrations depend on the moon sighting, expected around March 19th or 20th. Muslims worldwide will offer prayers, share feasts, and exchange gifts. This festival of breaking the fast emphasizes gratitude, unity, and charity. Homes will glow with lights as communities come together.

title: India’s 2027 vision is clear — except when it comes to Virat, Rohit
content: Despite clear long-term ambitions from coaching and selection staff, uncertainty looms over the ODI futures of Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma. While both superstars are performing well, the Indian cricket board needs to initiate direct dialogue regarding their inclusion in the 2027 World Cup plans. This lack of clarity, in the current scenario, hurts the dressing room more than it helps promote competitiveness.

title: Sharjeel Imam walks out of jail after over 6 years; Delhi court imposes tight curbs
content: Sharjeel Imam has been granted interim bail for 10 days to attend his brother's wedding and visit his ailing mother. The court imposed strict conditions, including no media interaction and limited contact with case-related individuals. Imam must surrender on March 30th after nearly six years in jail.

title: Big embarrassment for Canada: Top cop says no India link in transnational threats
content: Canadian police have found no evidence linking threats or criminal activity in the country to India, despite past diplomatic tensions. RCMP commissioner Mike Duheme stated ongoing investigations do not connect alleged intimidation cases to any foreign entity. This comes as diplomatic ties between Canada and India show signs of improvement.

title: Watch: Australian PM heckled at Eid prayers in Sydney for supporting Israel
content: Tensions flared at an Eid prayer gathering in Western Sydney as a few attendees heckled Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, demanding their removal. Shouts of "genocide supporters" echoed, reflecting community anger over the government's Gaza response and Islamophobia.

title: 'India can end ongoing war': RSS chief calls for global harmony as Iran war escalate
content: RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat asserted that India possesses the capability to end the Middle East conflict, noting global recognition of its peace-promoting role. He attributed wars to selfish interests and dominance, advocating for harmony and India's traditional philosophy of interconnectedness to restore global balance through dharma.

title: Rajasthan Royals cautioned over Sooryavanshi: Dravid-like mentor key this season
content: Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan urges caution around teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's IPL 2026 hype, acknowledging his potential while highlighting upcoming challenges. Pathan believes Vaibhav's consistent scoring across formats proves his 'dum,' suggesting this season could be his breakout.  Mental resilience and adaptation will be key for the 15-year-old prodigy.

title: Actress Nimisha Nair alleges harassment after 'drunk men' stop her cab in Mumbai; shares video
content: In a shocking incident in Mumbai, actress Nimisha Nair recounted a harrowing experience where she and her Uber driver were confronted by two men on a scooter. The duo aggressively impeded their vehicle on the busy Western Express Highway, threatening the driver and attempting to force open the doors. Read the full article to know more in detail here.

title: From Bombay to Mumbai: The deeper constitutional meaning of renaming cities in India
content: Renaming Indian cities is more than administrative change; it's a constitutional expression of identity and memory. While restoring indigenous names, the process must balance historical continuity with evolving cultural values. This requires transparency, public consultation, and careful management of practical implications to ensure inclusion and strengthen national belonging.

title: Mamata vs EC: Bengal govt moves Calcutta high court against transfers of IAS, IPS
content: The Trinamool Congress has filed a petition in the Calcutta High Court. They are challenging the Election Commission's decision to transfer several IAS and IPS officers in West Bengal. This action followed the announcement of assembly polls. The party questions the EC's authority to make these transfers without state government consultation. The Chief Minister has strongly criticized the move.

title: Bollywood-style jailbreak, but script failed: Inmate rats out fugitive after helping him escape
content: A daring escape from Shajapur jail saw a prisoner scale a 20-foot wall using improvised tools after ten days of meticulous observation. Despite injuring his leg during the jump, the fugitive managed to travel hundreds of kilometres to Chhattisgarh. However, his freedom was short-lived as a special police team tracked him down and apprehended him in a dramatic raid.

title: LPG crisis: India turns to US to secure supplies as Strait of Hormuz disruptions continue to bite
content: As per an S&P Global Energy report, India is moving to broaden its sources of LPG imports, stepping up refinery output and intensifying diplomatic engagement as it works to maintain a steady supply of cooking fuel for households.

title: 'Destiny got me here': Samson on Dhoni as CSK drops 1st Sanju-MSD video | Watch
content: Sanju Samson's long-held dream of playing alongside MS Dhoni has materialized as he joins Chennai Super Kings for IPL 2026.  This move follows Samson's stellar performance, earning him Player of the Tournament in the T20 World Cup 2026, where he set new Indian batting records.  Fans eagerly anticipate this exciting new chapter.

title: KVS Admission 2026: Registration begins, direct link here
content: The Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan (KVS) has opened online registrations for Balvatika and Class 1 admissions for 2026, starting March 20 and continuing until April 2. Parents can apply through the official portal, ensuring timely submission to avoid technical issues. Provisional admission lists will be released from April 8, 2026, across various categories, including RTE and service quotas.

title: IPL 2026: Complete list of injured and unavailable players
content: IPL 2026 faces a challenge with numerous player injuries. Key bowlers like Harshit Rana and Nathan Ellis are out for the season. Sam Curran and Jack Edwards will also miss the entire tournament. Pat Cummins and Josh Hazlewood will be absent for initial matches. Lockie Ferguson and Mitchell Starc's early availability is uncertain. Matheesha Pathirana is also sidelined.

title: MLB trade rumors: Braves linked with Yankees shortstop in $3.4M deal
content: Atlanta Braves are reportedly eyeing New York Yankees shortstop Anthony Volpe in a potential trade. This move addresses Atlanta's shortstop needs during Ha-Seong Kim's recovery and New York's dissatisfaction with Volpe's offensive output. The deal could see Mauricio Dubón head to the Bronx, offering defensive flexibility for the Yankees while Atlanta gains a younger, controllable talent.

title: NBA debut to March Madness: Sandfort surprises brother in Nebraska win

title: 31 Rajya Sabha MPs billionaires, 73 face criminal cases: ADR analysis
content: analysis of affidavits of 229 out of 233 Rajya Sabha MPs shows that 73 (32%) MPs have declared criminal cases against themselves and 31 (14%) are billionaires . one MP has declared a case of murder, four have attempts to murder, and three have crimes against women .
